identity.issuer_role: Project systems-research and technical publication body
identity.title: PROMETHEUS Forge: Live ServerForge Case Study
identity.subject: EDEN local execution, PROMETHEUS governance, ServerForge observability, and public proof delivery
identity.scope_note: The report establishes observable behavior and evidence lineage without claiming academic accreditation, exposing private orchestration, or disclosing patent-sensitive implementation details.

observed_server.name: PROMETHEUS Forge — Live Case Study
observed_server.function: Persistent observability, proof indexing, incident lineage, and judge-facing case-study publication for the PROMETHEUS competition campaign.
observed_server.observation_basis: Two creator-supplied mobile screenshots showing the server channel architecture and an application-authored campaign receipt publication.
observed_server.capture_state: The live-case-study channel displayed one application publication and one online member at the moment of capture. This is an observed state, not a claim of continuous uptime.

evidence_objects.title: PROMETHEUS Forge channel architecture
evidence_objects.observation: The server separates orientation, live case-study publication, proof indexing, mission control, adversarial work, ProofGrid, bridge control, telemetry, and incident/rollback operations.
evidence_objects.significance: Functional channel separation supports traceability and reduces ambiguity between narrative, proof, operations, and recovery records.

evidence_objects.title: Prometheus Server Forge deployment receipt
evidence_objects.observation: The Prometheus Server Forge application published a PROMETHEUS V-1.1.1 LIVE campaign message stating that HYDRA gates completed and the ServerForge deployment was promoted.
evidence_objects.significance: The message binds a named campaign, promotion statement, application identity, timestamp, and receipt digest into a persistent external observability surface.

evidence_objects.title: Executable Command-to-Proof implementation
evidence_objects.observation: The public repository contains the executable mission, replay guard, tests, ProofGrid receipts, Capability Genome, interface, and CI validation paths.
evidence_objects.significance: The Discord evidence is corroborative rather than standing alone; executable artifacts and repository history provide the primary inspection surface.

evidence_objects.title: GitHub Pages operator console
evidence_objects.observation: A clean GitHub runner fetched the deployed HTML, JavaScript, case-study data, and public ProofGrid receipt and returned a live verification pass.
evidence_objects.significance: The judge-facing evidence surface is independently reachable when the EDEN local machine is offline.

observed_receipt.statement: completed the HYDRA gates and promoted the ServerForge deployment
observed_receipt.forensic_note: The Discord-displayed local minute and the UTC-form campaign identifier are consistent with a possible UTC-7 relationship. This is a timestamp-consistency observation, not a claim about the device timezone.
observed_receipt.separation_note: This is the ServerForge deployment receipt observed in Discord. It is distinct from the current ProofGrid mission receipt published by the competition repository.

execution_chain.label: 01 · EDEN
execution_chain.name: Local execution body
execution_chain.detail: Windows-based project workspace, command environment, tools, repository access, and executable runtime used to perform the engineering work.

execution_chain.label: 02 · CODEX
execution_chain.name: Implementation accelerator
execution_chain.detail: Worked against the real competition repository to implement, debug, integrate, test, and harden the executable package and public interface.

execution_chain.label: 03 · PROMETHEUS
execution_chain.name: Command-to-Proof authority
execution_chain.detail: Admitted the mission, evaluated routes, reproduced failure, denied unsupported promotion, changed behavior, verified evidence, and authorized the proven state.

execution_chain.label: 04 · SERVERFORGE
execution_chain.name: Observability and case-study transport
execution_chain.detail: Published sanitized campaign events, deployment receipts, operational state, recovery context, and evidence lineage to the Discord case-study server.

execution_chain.label: 05 · PUBLIC NODE
execution_chain.name: Independent proof surface
execution_chain.detail: GitHub Pages publishes the deterministic operator console, ProofGrid receipts, Capability Genome, case study, and judge documentation independently of EDEN uptime.

methodology.detail: Record only visible server structure, message content, identifiers, timestamps, and application identity from the supplied captures.
Keep the ServerForge deployment receipt distinct from the ProofGrid mission receipt and distinguish Discord observation from repository verification.
Cross-reference the observed deployment narrative against executable repository artifacts, CI results, public receipts, and live-node verification.
State only what each evidence object supports; do not infer hidden channel contents, continuous uptime, undisclosed integrations, or proprietary mechanisms.
Publish roles, observable transitions, verification outcomes, and sanitized lineage while withholding private prompts, credentials, paths, routing logic, and patent-sensitive mechanisms.

timeline.event: Defined the public field-report method, claim boundary, and evidence classification for the competition case study.
Real local competition repository and executable development environment staged.
CLI, deterministic mission, tests, proof artifacts, operator console, and deployment controls implemented against the repository.
Candidate routes evaluated; bounded replay failure reproduced; unsupported promotion denied.
Idempotent replay guard changed runtime behavior from exception to safe identical replay handling.
Five tests and eight public evidence artifacts verified; mission receipt sealed.
Discord campaign identifier records the HYDRA-SERVERFORGE deployment event; the visible message reports HYDRA gate completion and deployment promotion.
Sanitized campaign state and receipt lineage persisted to the live case-study observability surface.
Capability reuse verified and promotion authorized for the evidence-supported state.
Clean runner verified the deployed operator console, JavaScript, canonical agent data, telemetry, case-study data, IP boundary, proof totals, and public receipt.

findings: The case study is attached to a real persistent Discord server rather than a purely illustrative web mockup.
A named application, Prometheus Server Forge, published a campaign-specific deployment record to #live-case-study.
The visible server architecture separates onboarding, narrative publication, proof indexing, mission operations, adversarial records, ProofGrid, transport, telemetry, and rollback concerns.
The observed ServerForge deployment receipt and the public ProofGrid mission receipt are separate evidence objects with separate purposes.
The Discord evidence is corroborated by executable repository code, tests, artifact hashes, CI history, and an independently reachable public node.
PROMETHEUS demonstrates fail-closed behavior: the system records a failure and denies its own promotion before repair and verification.
ServerForge contributes credibility through externalized operational lineage, but release authority remains with the PROMETHEUS proof chain and Build Truth.
EDEN demonstrates that the work occurred in a real local engineering environment; the public node demonstrates that inspection does not depend on that machine remaining online.
Ghost Atlas credibility comes from disciplined object separation, explicit limitations, reproducible verification paths, and refusal to present private architecture as publicly proven fact.
The public case study communicates institutional engineering maturity without disclosing the mechanisms that constitute protected intellectual property.
limitations: The screenshots establish the observed Discord state at capture time; they do not independently establish continuous availability before or after capture.
Collapsed Discord categories prove category presence only. Their internal channels and contents are not inferred.
The ServerForge message proves that the application published the displayed statement and receipt; the repository and CI provide the stronger technical corroboration for executable behavior.
No claim is made that Discord itself executes PROMETHEUS. Discord is the observability and case-study publication surface.
Ghost Atlas Institute is presented as the project’s systems-research and technical publication body, not as an external accreditation claim.

credibility_model.test: Can the origin of the claim be identified?
credibility_model.implementation: Evidence IDs, application identity, campaign ID, timestamps, receipt digests, repository history, and live-node checks.

credibility_model.test: Are different systems and receipts kept distinct?
credibility_model.implementation: ServerForge deployment receipt, ProofGrid mission receipt, Discord observation, executable verification, and public presentation are separately labeled.

credibility_model.test: Does more than one evidence surface support the narrative?
credibility_model.implementation: Screenshots, repository artifacts, CI, public receipts, and GitHub Pages verification converge on the same bounded claims.

credibility_model.test: Does uncertainty reduce the claim rather than inflate it?
credibility_model.implementation: Unexpanded categories are not invented; uptime is not generalized; private transports are not described; unsupported promotion remains blocked.

credibility_model.test: Can a judge inspect beyond the story?
credibility_model.implementation: Executable CLI, tests, receipt verification, evidence verification, claims verification, submission check, and machine-readable public artifacts.

credibility_model.test: Is credibility achieved without surrendering proprietary internals?
credibility_model.implementation: Observable behavior and evidence are public; prompts, credentials, routes, local paths, recursive mechanisms, and patent-sensitive algorithms remain withheld.

disclosed: Canonical system responsibilities and state transitions
Observed Discord channel architecture and application publication
Campaign and receipt identifiers already visible in the supplied evidence
Bounded failure and repair behavior needed to understand the demonstration
Public test totals, artifact totals, receipt lineage, and live-node verification
withheld: Credentials, bot tokens, webhooks, server IDs, and private invitation links
Private machine paths, local state, and unpublished repositories
Proprietary routing, recursive self-modification, and autonomous recovery internals

recording_sequence: Show the Operator Console case-study heading and Ghost Atlas Institute report identity.
Show the five-node EDEN → Codex → PROMETHEUS → ServerForge → Public Node chain.
Show the sanitized Discord server architecture and identify #live-case-study, #proof-index, #telemetry, and #incidents-and-rollback.
Show the ServerForge deployment receipt exhibit and explicitly distinguish it from the ProofGrid mission receipt.
Show the findings and credibility model, then open the machine-readable ProofGrid receipt or Judge Challenge Mode.
Close by stating that the case study proves observable engineering lineage while preserving protected implementation details.
